scope: Stage 5 crisis response, hotline filtering, FSM and share QR

automated_evidence.qr_reference_check: Version 3-L/mask 0 matrices matched qrcode 8.2 byte-for-byte for two URLs; qrcode was installed only under /tmp for verification and is not a project/runtime dependency.
automated_evidence.hotline_live_verification: 12/12 number-specific official URLs returned HTTP 200 and contained the exact normalized number in visible HTML/PDF text; hashes and final URLs are in stage5-hotline-verification.json.

safety_controls: Full user message is used only for local bank-alias matching; client red_flags are discarded, so neither field is inserted into the Rescuer prompt.
Every displayed action, headline, reassurance and closing is deterministic reviewed copy; no AI-generated crisis prose is rendered.
AI output with URL, email, unknown short/long phone-like sequence, unsafe OTP/link/remote-app/payment instruction, missing step key or wrong shape is rejected wholesale.
Hotline IDs are allowlisted per safety step and rendered from static dataclasses, not copied from AI text.
Police 113 remains in the public verified table but is never automatically attached to the four standard situations because immediate danger was not established.
The current official Ministry article identifies 156 for both voice and message reports; the older unverified 5656 record was removed rather than retained by assumption.
Gemini network/parse/guardrail failure and RESCUE_AI_ENABLED=false retain useful deterministic playbooks.
QR target must be HTTPS in production and its hostname must be in SHARE_ALLOWED_HOSTS; attacker query URLs and Host headers are rejected/ignored.
adversarial_regressions: Unknown 0909 phone and known number on the wrong step
Arbitrary https URL and email contact
Instruction to click link, install remote control, transfer more money or pay recovery fee
Generic use of 113 without immediate-danger evidence
Host-header and BASE_URL QR origin injection

hotline_review.source_policy: Each entry requires an official number-specific URL, exact evidence quote containing the normalized number, and checked date; remove rather than guess.
hotline_review.residual_risk: A second human should still re-check all official pages/cards before the live demo and every 90 days because organizations can change numbers.

final_independent_review.follow_up: Reviewer noted client red_flag labels could carry private text; the route now discards them before prompt construction and a regression test proves neither message text nor labels reach Gemini.

findings_remediated: No model-generated reassurance or other crisis prose is rendered; all visible copy is deterministic.
Generic homepage references were replaced by ten bank contact pages/documents and number-specific government articles for 113/156.
A reproducible live verifier now records HTTP status, exact number match and content SHA-256; 12/12 passed.
The older 5656 assumption and four unproven/outdated bank records were removed; MSB was corrected from an old number to the current contact-page number.
Free-form AI actions could pass structural step-key checks.
URLs, email, 911 and broader phone-like values were not fail-closed.
113 was attached to a generic police-reporting step.
QR could derive an attacker-controlled Host when BASE_URL was empty.
No recorded Stage 5 safety self-assessment existed.

residual_risks: Browser cannot save directly into iOS Photos without the native share sheet/user gesture.
Hotline freshness still requires human operational review.
AI reassurance is non-authoritative and falls back entirely if any guardrail check fails.
